overview: Copies text from a reference file (second input) into a destination file (first input) by matching text unit ids (`TextUnit.getName()`). Works with any filter that produces unique names for text units (e.g., Properties Filter). For monolingual reference files, the source text is copied into the target; for multilingual reference files (e.g., PO), the target text is used.

markAsTranslateNo.help:
When a text unit in the destination file has a matching entry in the reference file, this flag marks the text unit as **non-translatable** after the copy. Text units without a match are left unchanged.

Useful when the copied content represents finalized translations that should not be modified further downstream in the pipeline.
notes: Only applies to text units that have a match in the reference file. Unmatched entries retain their original translatable state.
Entries already marked as non-translatable in the destination file are skipped entirely — they are never modified by this step.

markAsApproved.help:
When a text unit has a matching entry in the reference file, sets the `approved` target property to `yes` after copying. This signals to downstream steps and tools (e.g., XLIFF editors) that the translation has been reviewed.

Text units without a match retain their original `approved` state.
notes: Only applies to matched text units. Unmatched entries keep their existing approval state.

processingNotes: The reference file is read entirely into an in-memory table indexed by text unit name before processing the destination file.

For **monolingual** reference files (e.g., `.properties`), the **source** text of the reference is copied into the **target** of the destination. For **multilingual** files (e.g., PO), the **target** text is used.
The complete content of text units is copied — no adjustment is made for segmentation. Use the **Desegmentation Step** beforehand if needed.
No adjustment is made for inline codes when copying between different file formats — results depend on whether inline codes are present and the formats involved.
After processing each file pair, entries in the reference table that were **not matched** are listed as warnings (these may simply be obsolete entries).
No warning is generated when a destination entry has no match in the reference file.
Entries in the destination file marked as non-translatable are **never modified**, even if a match exists.
When working with input lists, files must be paired: the first file in Input List 1 corresponds to the first file in Input List 2, and so on.
limitations: The reference file is loaded entirely into memory — very large reference files may cause memory issues.
No inline code adjustment is performed when reference and destination use different file formats.
Both files must have text units with **unique** names (e.g., unique `resname` in XLIFF). Duplicate names will cause unpredictable matching.
No segmentation adjustment is made — segmented entries are copied as-is.

examples.title: Copy translations from properties to properties
examples.description: Use a translated `.properties` file as the reference to populate targets in a destination `.properties` file. Since properties files are monolingual, the source text from the reference is copied into the target of matching text units in the destination.
examples.config:
```yaml
step: id-based-copy
markAsTranslateNo: false
markAsApproved: true
```

examples.title: Copy from PO file reference
examples.description: When using a multilingual PO file as the reference, the **target** text (translated string) from the PO file is copied into matching text units in the destination file, and matched entries are marked as approved.
